bug_report.summary: Unexpected glyph/marker appears in editor gutter near line 1 (rendering artifact)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the “Welcome to Zettlr!” document (or any document showing the YAML frontmatter section).
Look at the left gutter/line-number area near the first line in the editor.
expected_behaviors: The gutter should show only valid line numbers and intended markers (no stray characters).
actual_behaviors: A new/changed small character/marker (looks like an extra glyph under/near the line number) appears in the gutter compared to before, indicating an unintended rendering change.

bug_report.summary: Document word/character counts change after pilcrow cleanup (likely counting visual pilcrow/whitespace markers as content)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Zettlr and enable Editor setting to show whitespace/line endings (pilcrows).
Paste the provided long mixed-content text block (regular lines + fenced code block + regular lines).
Observe the word count/character count shown in the UI (top-right stats and/or status bar).
expected_behaviors: Word/character counts should reflect the actual document text only and remain consistent between builds for the same pasted content.
Visual whitespace/line-ending markers (e.g., pilcrow ¶) should not be counted as document characters/words.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the displayed statistics differ from the before-build for the same step/content (e.g., word count and character count are higher in the after screenshot: ~175 words / 958 characters vs ~168 words / 920 characters in before).

bug_report.summary: Preview pane heading marker rendering differs (left-edge artifact/shift on green heading indicator)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Split View tutorial content with two preview panes visible (as shown).
Look at the heading marker (green #) next to the “Split View” heading in the top-right pane and the “Helpful Links” heading in the bottom-right pane.
expected_behaviors: Heading markers in the preview should render identically between builds (no added artifacts or shifts) unless explicitly part of the intended change.
actual_behaviors: After change: the left edge of the green heading indicator shows a small rendering difference (appears shifted/with an extra colored edge) in both preview panes compared to before change.

bug_report.summary: After creating a new document, typing/Enter can edit the previously open document instead of the new file (focus/active document incorrect)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new blank Markdown document (Document1) via File → New file… and save it.
Click into the editor area and type: See [project plan](https://example.invalid/plan)
Observe which document content actually changes (Split View tutorial file vs Document1).
expected_behaviors: After creating and selecting Document1, keyboard input (typing and Enter) should modify Document1 only.
Previously open/tutorial documents (e.g., “Split View”) should not be modified unless explicitly activated. ამ
actual_behaviors: Before change: the typed text and subsequent Enter/newline appear inside the existing “Split View” content (Split View becomes modified).
After change: the typed text appears in Document1 (Document1 becomes modified).

bug_report.summary: Drag-selecting text across lines fails (selection collapses to caret; shows “0 selected”) when whitespace markers are enabled
steps_to_reproduce: Enable whitespace/line-ending visualization (Highlight whitespace / Show whitespace).
In a Markdown document, type two lines of text (as in the scenario) and press Enter after each line.
Click at the beginning of the first line.
Click-drag to the end of the second line to select both lines.
expected_behaviors: The dragged region should become highlighted and the UI should indicate a non-zero selection (i.e., the text is selected).
Whitespace/line-ending markers (pilcrows) should not prevent or cancel selection.
actual_behaviors: After change, the selection does not appear/highlight, and the selection indicator shows “0 selected” even after performing the drag selection.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)
